briefs.topic: Trial Master File / Essential-Documents catalog for a sponsor-investigator small-site trial (STARTUP / CONDUCT / CLOSEOUT), mapped to ICH E6(R3) Appendix C and 21 CFR
briefs.summary: This catalog enumerates the essential documents/records required across the lifecycle of a US sponsor-investigator early-phase (e.g., Phase 2) trial, organized as STARTUP, CONDUCT, and CLOSEOUT and mapped to the ICH E6(R3) Appendix C ("Essential Records for the Conduct of a Clinical Trial") framework that superseded the E6(R2) §8 essential-documents list at Step 4 adoption (Jan 2025). E6(R3) deliberately shifted terminology from "documents" to "records" to encompass data and dynamic/electronic content, and applies risk-proportionality: the nature and extent of records depend on trial design and materiality, so not every listed item is mandatory for every trial. The governing US legal frame is 21 CFR Parts 312 (IND), 50 (informed consent), 56 (IRB), and 54 (financial disclosure); FDA form numbers (1571, 1572, 3454/3455, MedWatch 3500A) are load-bearing and cited per document. A defining feature of the sponsor-investigator model (21 CFR 312.3) is that one physician holds both sponsor and investigator obligations, so documents that would normally flow BETWEEN two entities (e.g., 1572 from investigator to sponsor, IND safety reports from sponsor to investigators) collapse to self-directed obligations plus the outward flow to FDA and IRB — but the obligations themselves do not disappear. Several functions are non-delegable to software and are flagged as gating: administration of informed consent to a patient, IRB/IEC ethical judgment, SAE causality/expectedness determination, and the sponsor-investigator's signature attestations (1571/1572). Confirmed regulatory requirements are distinguished from interpretive/best-practice items (e.g., DSMB charters and monitoring plans are conditionally required or guidance-driven, not universally mandated by regulation).

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Clinical protocol + amendments
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: defines objectives, design, methodology, statistics, eligibility, safety/stopping rules; the scientific and operational contract for the trial. Owner: sponsor-investigator (authors/approves; signs protocol signature page). Receives: FDA (as part of IND), IRB, DSMB, site staff, pharma/CRO partner. Confirmed requirement. Phase 2 protocols must be full-detail (not the abbreviated Phase 1 outline).
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.23(a)(6); 21 CFR 312.30 (protocol amendments); ICH E6(R3) §7 + Appendix C (before-trial); E6(R2) §8.2.2

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Protocol synopsis
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: 2-5 page structured abstract of the protocol for rapid review by IRB/DSMB/partners and feasibility screening. Owner: sponsor-investigator. Receives: IRB, DSMB, pharma partner, prospective sites. Best-practice/derived component of the protocol; not separately mandated by CFR but expected by ICH E6(R3) §7 protocol contents and universal in practice.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §7 (protocol); interpretive — no standalone CFR mandate

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Investigator's Brochure (IB)
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: compiles clinical + nonclinical data on the investigational product relevant to human study; the safety/rationale reference for investigators. Owner: sponsor (sponsor-investigator prepares, or receives from the pharma manufacturer who supplies drug). Receives: investigators, IRB, FDA (in IND). For a marketed drug studied under an IND, a current package insert may substitute per 312.55.

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] IND application — Form FDA 1571 (cover)
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: the FDA authorization vehicle to ship/administer an investigational drug across state lines; 1571 is the required cover form binding the whole IND (CMC, pharm/tox, prior human experience, protocol, IB, 1572s). Owner: sponsor-investigator (signs as sponsor). Receives: FDA/CDER (or CBER). Confirmed. 30-day safety clock under 312.40.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.23 (content/format); Form FDA 1571; 21 CFR 312.20; 21 CFR 312.40 (may-proceed)

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Statement of Investigator — Form FDA 1572
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: investigator's binding commitment to conduct the trial per protocol, GCP, and 21 CFR 312.60; lists site, IRB, sub-investigators, facilities. Owner: investigator (in sponsor-investigator model, self-executed). Receives: sponsor (retained; NOT routinely submitted to FDA except within the IND). Confirmed.

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Financial disclosure — Form FDA 3454 (certification) / 3455 (disclosure)
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: certify absence of, or disclose, clinical-investigator financial interests that could bias the study; collected BEFORE participation. Owner: sponsor collects; each investigator/sub-investigator completes. Receives: sponsor retains; submitted to FDA only with a later marketing application. Confirmed. Covers period of study through 1 year after completion.

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Informed Consent Form (ICF) + any assent/short form
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: the document by which a subject voluntarily confirms willingness to participate after disclosure of the 8 basic + additional elements; legal + ethical prerequisite to enrollment. Owner: sponsor-investigator drafts; IRB approves; investigator administers. Receives: IRB (approval), subject (signed copy), sponsor file. Confirmed. Administration to a patient is NON-DELEGABLE to software.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 50.20, 50.25, 50.27; 45 CFR 46.116 (if federally funded); ICH E6(R3) §4/Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.2.3

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] IRB submission package
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: complete dossier enabling IRB initial review — protocol, ICF, IB, recruitment materials, investigator CV, 1572, budget/COI, grant/agreement. Owner: sponsor-investigator. Receives: IRB/IEC. Confirmed as prerequisite; IRB review itself is non-delegable ethical judgment.

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] IRB approval letter (initial) + approved-document set
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: documents IRB favorable opinion, version-stamped approved ICF/protocol, and approval/expiration dates; required before any subject may be enrolled. Owner: IRB issues. Receives: sponsor-investigator (files), FDA (available on request). Confirmed.

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] IRB roster / statement of compliance / IRB assurance
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: evidence the reviewing IRB is constituted and operates per 21 CFR 56 (membership, quorum, GCP). Owner: IRB. Receives: sponsor-investigator file. Confirmed as an essential record; often a GCP-compliance statement plus membership list.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 56.107; ICH E6(R3) Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.2.8

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] FDA IND acknowledgment / safe-to-proceed correspondence
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: FDA letter assigning the IND number and confirming the study may proceed after the 30-day review (or notice of clinical hold). Owner: FDA issues. Receives: sponsor-investigator. Confirmed.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.20; 21 CFR 312.40; 21 CFR 312.42 (clinical hold)

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] DSMB/DMC charter + membership
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: defines the independent Data Monitoring Committee's mandate, stopping guidelines, meeting cadence, and unblinding procedures for interim safety/efficacy review. Owner: sponsor-investigator establishes; DSMB members adopt. Receives: DSMB, IRB (informational), FDA (in IND if applicable). INTERPRETIVE/CONDITIONAL: not universally mandated; expected for higher-risk or blinded early-phase trials per FDA DMC guidance.
obligations_or_content.citation: FDA Guidance 'Establishment and Operation of Clinical Trial Data Monitoring Committees' (2006); ICH E6(R3) §5 (sponsor oversight); 21 CFR 312.23(a)(6)(iii)(g) (monitoring in protocol) — conditional

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Monitoring plan (risk-based)
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: defines sponsor monitoring strategy — scope, methods (on-site/central/RBM), frequency, source-data-verification approach, escalation. Owner: sponsor (sponsor-investigator or delegated micro-CRO/monitor). Receives: monitors, retained in sponsor TMF. Confirmed as a sponsor oversight obligation under E6(R3); form is risk-proportionate.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §6.9 (monitoring); FDA Guidance 'Oversight of Clinical Investigations — Risk-Based Monitoring' (2013, rev.); 21 CFR 312.50, 312.56

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Safety management plan / pharmacovigilance plan
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: operationalizes AE/SAE collection, causality/expectedness assessment workflow, IND safety-report timelines (7-day fatal/life-threatening unexpected; 15-day serious unexpected), MedWatch routing, and reconciliation with the pharma partner. Owner: sponsor-investigator (sponsor safety obligations). Receives: safety/PV function, investigators, FDA (reports). Confirmed obligation; the written plan is best-practice.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.32 (IND safety reporting); 21 CFR 312.64(b) (investigator SAE reporting to sponsor); FDA Guidance on IND Safety Reporting (2012/2015); ICH E2A

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Delegation-of-authority / task-delegation log
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: records which trial tasks the investigator delegated to which qualified staff, with signatures, dates, and training evidence; the accountability spine for site conduct. Owner: investigator/sponsor-investigator. Receives: site file (ISF), available to monitor/FDA. Confirmed essential record.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §2 (investigator responsibilities) + Appendix C; 21 CFR 312.53(c), 312.60; E6(R2) §8.3.24 (signature sheet)

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] CVs, medical licenses, GCP training certificates (PI + sub-investigators + key staff)
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: evidences qualification and current training to conduct the trial. Owner: each individual; investigator compiles. Receives: sponsor (in IND via 1572 attachments), IRB, site file. Confirmed. Sponsor-investigator CV + license accompany the 1572 in the IND.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.53(c)(2) (CV/qualifications); ICH E6(R3) §2 + Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.2.10; FDA GCP training expectation

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Central/local laboratory certifications (CLIA/CAP) + accreditations
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: documents the analyzing lab is certified to produce reliable clinical results. Owner: laboratory. Receives: sponsor-investigator file, IRB, FDA on request. Confirmed essential record.
obligations_or_content.citation: 42 CFR Part 493 (CLIA); ICH E6(R3) Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.2.12; 21 CFR 312.23(a)(6)(iii) (facilities)

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Laboratory normal ranges / reference values
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: enables interpretation of lab results and clinical-significance judgments against validated reference intervals; version/date controlled. Owner: laboratory. Receives: sponsor-investigator file, investigators. Confirmed essential record; updates during trial must be captured.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.2.11; 21 CFR 312.62 (records supporting data)

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Clinical Trial Agreement (CTA) + budget
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: the executed contract among sponsor, site/institution, and (where applicable) pharma partner/CRO — scope, indemnification, IP, publication, payment schedule, and the budget. Owner: contracting parties. Receives: all signatories; sponsor + site files. Confirmed as a financing/agreement essential record.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §5 (sponsor, financing) + Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.2.6 (financial aspects) / §8.2.4-8.2.5 (agreements); contract law (not a single CFR)

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Feasibility questionnaire / site qualification (pre-study visit report)
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: assesses site capability, patient population, equipment, and staff before selection; documents sponsor's investigator-selection due diligence. Owner: sponsor (or pharma partner) issues; site completes. Receives: sponsor selection file. Confirmed as sponsor obligation to select qualified investigators; the questionnaire form is best-practice.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.53(a) (selecting investigators); ICH E6(R3) §5 + §6.9 (pre-trial monitoring/qualification); E6(R2) §8.2.20 (pre-study monitoring visit report)

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Regulatory binder / Investigator Site File (ISF) index
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: the master index/table-of-contents controlling the location and version of every essential record held at the site; the auditable map of the ISF. Owner: investigator (site); mirrors sponsor TMF. Receives: monitors, FDA inspectors. Confirmed — E6(R3) requires records be readily identifiable and retrievable.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §C.2 (management of essential records) + Appendix C; 21 CFR 312.62 (investigator recordkeeping)

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Source-document templates / source worksheets
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: pre-formatted worksheets capturing original observations (vitals, visit notes, eligibility checklists) to ensure ALCOA-C source data. Owner: sponsor-investigator/site designs. Receives: site (primary), monitor verifies. Confirmed as needed to support data integrity; templates themselves are best-practice tooling.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §6 (data governance, ALCOA++) + Appendix C; 21 CFR 312.62(b) (case histories/source)

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Blank CRF / eCRF (case report form, version-controlled)
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: the instrument for recording protocol-required data per subject for transmission to the sponsor. Owner: sponsor designs; investigator completes during conduct. Receives: sponsor/data management. Confirmed essential record (blank version filed at startup).
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.62(b); ICH E6(R3) §6 + Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.2.13 (sample CRF)

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Drug/IP accountability log (initialized) + investigational product management plan
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: tracks receipt, storage conditions, dispensing, return, and reconciliation of investigational product; controls chain of custody. Owner: investigator/pharmacy; sponsor supplies drug + shipping records. Receives: site file, monitor, FDA on request. Confirmed.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.57 (sponsor disposition records), 312.61, 312.62(a) (investigator drug disposition); ICH E6(R3) Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.2.15-8.2.16

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] IP labeling / sample labels
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: documents investigational-use caution statement and required labeling for the supplied drug. Owner: sponsor/pharma manufacturer. Receives: site file. Confirmed essential record.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.6 (label — 'Caution: New Drug—Limited by Federal law to investigational use'); ICH E6(R3) Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.2.14

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Randomization scheme, blinding procedures, and code-break/unblinding SOP
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: documents allocation method and the sealed-code emergency-unblinding procedure. Owner: sponsor (statistician). Receives: site (sealed), pharmacy, DSMB. Confirmed for randomized/blinded designs; conditional on design.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §6 + Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.2.17-8.2.19 (randomization/decoding); 21 CFR 312.23(a)(6) (statistical methods)

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Recruitment / advertising materials (IRB-approved)
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: any subject-facing recruitment content; must be IRB-reviewed as part of consent process. Owner: sponsor-investigator drafts; IRB approves. Receives: IRB, site file. Confirmed.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 50.25; 21 CFR 56.111; FDA Guidance 'Recruiting Study Subjects' (IRB review of advertising); ICH E6(R3) §4

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Insurance / indemnification certificate
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: evidence of compensation/insurance coverage for trial subjects where required by law or agreement. Owner: sponsor/institution. Receives: IRB, site file. Conditional/interpretive under US law (more explicit in EU/ICH); required where local law or IRB demands.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) Appendix C (insurance statement where applicable); E6(R2) §8.2.9; jurisdiction-dependent

obligations_or_content.item: [STARTUP] Manual of Operations / study procedures manual + lab/imaging manuals
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: operationalizes protocol into step-by-step site procedures, specimen handling, and instructions. Owner: sponsor-investigator. Receives: site staff, monitors. Best-practice; not separately CFR-mandated but supports GCP conduct.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §6.9/§7 (instructions to handle IP and materials); interpretive

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] Signed/executed informed consent forms (per subject)
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: dated proof each enrolled subject consented before any study procedure. Owner: investigator obtains. Receives: site file (originals), subject (copy). Confirmed essential during-trial record; consenting act is NON-DELEGABLE.

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] Screening log, enrollment log, subject identification code list
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: accounts for all screened/enrolled subjects and links coded IDs to identities (kept confidential). Owner: investigator. Receives: site file; monitor verifies. Confirmed.

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] Source documents (completed) + completed CRFs/eCRFs and correction/audit trail
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: the primary data and the reported data, plus documented corrections preserving the original entry. Owner: investigator/site records; sponsor holds CRF data. Receives: sponsor/data management; monitor SDV; FDA on inspection. Confirmed.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.62(b); ICH E6(R3) §6 (data integrity, ALCOA++) + Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.3.13-8.3.14

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] Monitoring visit reports + monitoring log + follow-up letters
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: documents each monitoring visit, findings, and issue resolution; evidence of sponsor oversight. Owner: sponsor/monitor (micro-CRO layer where used). Receives: sponsor TMF; site copy. Confirmed.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §6.9; 21 CFR 312.56 (review of ongoing investigations); E6(R2) §8.3.10

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] SAE report forms — investigator-to-sponsor (MedWatch 3500A or equivalent) + AE/SAE logs
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: investigator reports serious adverse events to the sponsor immediately (per protocol timelines) with causality assessment; feeds IND safety evaluation. Owner: investigator reports; sponsor receives/evaluates. Receives: sponsor (safety), then FDA/IRB as escalated. Confirmed. Causality/expectedness determination is NON-DELEGABLE clinical judgment.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.64(b) (investigator SAE to sponsor); Form FDA 3500A (MedWatch); ICH E2A; E6(R2) §8.3.16-8.3.18

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] IND safety reports — sponsor-to-FDA + to all investigators (7-day / 15-day)
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: expedited reporting of serious+unexpected+drug-related events; 7 calendar days for fatal/life-threatening unexpected, 15 days for other serious unexpected. Owner: sponsor-investigator (as sponsor). Receives: FDA + all participating investigators + IRB. Confirmed. In sponsor-investigator model this is a self-originated obligation, not received from an external sponsor.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.32(c); 21 CFR 312.55(b) (inform investigators); Form FDA 3500A / narrative; ICH E2A

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] Investigator's Brochure updates / revisions
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: keeps the safety reference current as new data emerge; at least annual review. Owner: sponsor. Receives: investigators, IRB, FDA (IND amendment). Confirmed.

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] Protocol amendments + IRB re-approval of amendments
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: documents and authorizes protocol changes; substantive changes require FDA amendment and IRB approval before implementation (except to eliminate immediate hazard). Owner: sponsor-investigator submits. Receives: FDA (312.30), IRB (56.110/56.108). Confirmed.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.30 (protocol amendments to FDA); 21 CFR 56.108(a), 56.110 (IRB review of changes); 21 CFR 312.66; ICH E6(R3) §7

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] IRB continuing review / annual re-approval + expedited approvals
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: ongoing IRB oversight at intervals appropriate to risk (historically ≤1 year; note 2018 Common Rule eliminated mandatory annual continuing review for some minimal-risk studies, but FDA 21 CFR 56 still requires it for FDA-regulated research). Owner: IRB. Receives: sponsor-investigator file. Confirmed.

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] IND Annual Report
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: yearly report to FDA within 60 days of the IND anniversary — summary of prior year's clinical/nonclinical progress, safety, subject accrual, and plans. Owner: sponsor-investigator. Receives: FDA. Confirmed.

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] DSMB/DMC meeting minutes + recommendations + interim analysis reports
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: documents independent safety reviews, unblinded interim results, and continue/modify/stop recommendations. Owner: DSMB; sponsor files recommendations (blinded). Receives: sponsor-investigator, IRB, FDA where applicable. Conditional on a DSMB existing for the trial.
obligations_or_content.citation: FDA DMC Guidance (2006); ICH E6(R3) §5 (oversight); interpretive/conditional

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] Drug accountability (ongoing), shipment/receipt records, temperature logs
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: continuous chain-of-custody and storage-condition evidence for investigational product. Owner: sponsor ships/records; investigator/pharmacy dispenses/records. Receives: site + sponsor files; monitor reconciles. Confirmed.

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] Protocol deviation / violation log + notes to file
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: records departures from protocol/GCP and their handling; supports data-integrity and CAPA assessment. Owner: investigator/sponsor. Receives: site + sponsor files, IRB (reportable deviations), monitor. Confirmed as an essential during-trial record.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §2/§6 + Appendix C; 21 CFR 312.66 (report changes/problems to IRB); 21 CFR 56.108(b) (unanticipated problems)

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] Correspondence log (sponsor/IRB/FDA/partner) + relevant communications
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: retains letters, emails, meeting notes with regulatory/ethical/operational significance. Owner: all parties; each files its own. Receives: TMF/ISF. Confirmed essential record.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.3.11

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] Updated CVs / licenses / training + delegation-log updates + new staff financial disclosures
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: keeps qualification and delegation records current as staff change. Owner: investigator/sponsor. Receives: site + sponsor files, IRB (new investigators via 1572 update). Confirmed.

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] Updated lab certifications / normal-range revisions
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: captures any change to lab accreditation or reference ranges during the trial for correct result interpretation. Owner: laboratory. Receives: sponsor-investigator file. Confirmed.
obligations_or_content.citation: 42 CFR Part 493; ICH E6(R3) Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.3.7-8.3.9

obligations_or_content.item: [CONDUCT] Signature sheet (updated) and site signature/initial log
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: maps every handwritten/electronic signature and initial used on trial records to a named, authorized person. Owner: investigator/site. Receives: site file, monitor. Confirmed.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §2 + Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.3.24; 21 CFR Part 11 (e-signatures if electronic)

obligations_or_content.item: [CLOSEOUT] Study closeout monitoring visit report
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: confirms all data collected/queries resolved, drug reconciled/returned, records complete and archived, and outstanding issues closed at the site. Owner: sponsor/monitor. Receives: sponsor TMF, site copy. Confirmed.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §6.9 + Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.4.1 (final trial close-out monitoring visit report)

obligations_or_content.item: [CLOSEOUT] Investigational product final reconciliation + return/destruction records
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: accounts for all dispensed/returned/destroyed drug and documents authorized destruction. Owner: investigator/pharmacy + sponsor. Receives: sponsor TMF, site file. Confirmed. Authorization of destruction is a gated sponsor decision.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.59 (disposition/return), 312.62(a); ICH E6(R3) Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.4.2-8.4.3

obligations_or_content.item: [CLOSEOUT] Notification to FDA of study completion / IND withdrawal or inactivation
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: informs FDA the study/IND is complete, withdrawn (312.38), or inactivated (312.45); final safety and disposition wrap-up. Owner: sponsor-investigator. Receives: FDA. Confirmed.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312.38 (withdrawal), 312.45 (inactivation), 312.33 (final annual/completion via annual report)

obligations_or_content.item: [CLOSEOUT] Final IRB notification of study completion
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: closes IRB oversight with a completion report + summary of results/AEs. Owner: sponsor-investigator. Receives: IRB. Confirmed.

obligations_or_content.item: [CLOSEOUT] Clinical Study Report (CSR)
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: the integrated final scientific + statistical report of the trial's methods and results. Owner: sponsor-investigator (with statistician). Receives: FDA (if used to support a marketing app), pharma partner, IRB summary. Confirmed as an essential after-trial record; format per ICH E3.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E3 (Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports); ICH E6(R3) Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.4.9; 21 CFR 312.33 (final report)

obligations_or_content.item: [CLOSEOUT] Treatment allocation / decoding / unblinding documentation
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: documents the final code-break so who received what is retrievable for analysis. Owner: sponsor/statistician. Receives: sponsor TMF, DSMB. Confirmed for blinded trials.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.4.7; 21 CFR 312.62

obligations_or_content.item: [CLOSEOUT] Audit certificate / statement (if independent audit performed)
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: attests an independent QA audit occurred. Owner: sponsor QA/auditor. Receives: sponsor TMF. Conditional — only when an audit was conducted.
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3) §5 (quality assurance/audit) + Appendix C; E6(R2) §8.4.8; interpretive/conditional

obligations_or_content.item: [CLOSEOUT] ClinicalTrials.gov registration + results information submission
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: statutory registration (within 21 days of first enrollment) and results posting (generally within 1 year of primary completion) for applicable clinical trials. Owner: responsible party (sponsor-investigator). Receives: NIH/NLM ClinicalTrials.gov; enforced by FDA. Confirmed for 'applicable clinical trials'; a Phase 2 drug trial typically qualifies.
obligations_or_content.citation: 42 CFR Part 11 (Final Rule); FDAAA 801 (Public Law 110-85 §801); 42 U.S.C. 282(j)

obligations_or_content.item: [CLOSEOUT] Record-retention statement + archival
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: documents the retention obligation and archive location for all essential records. Owner: sponsor-investigator + investigator. Receives: TMF/ISF (archived). Confirmed. FDA: retain 2 years after a marketing application is approved for the indication, or 2 years after IND discontinuation and FDA notification; ICH E6(R3) requires retention until at least 2 years after last marketing-application approval or discontinuation of clinical development.

obligations_or_content.item: [CLOSEOUT] Final subject screening/enrollment logs + final safety reconciliation
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: closes accountability for all subjects and reconciles the site AE/SAE database against the sponsor safety database. Owner: investigator + sponsor safety. Receives: sponsor TMF. Confirmed.

obligations_or_content.item: [CLOSEOUT] Final financial reconciliation / closeout of CTA payments
obligations_or_content.detail: Purpose: settles budget, per-subject payments, and pass-through costs; supports 1-year-post financial-disclosure window. Owner: sponsor + site/institution. Receives: contracting parties. Best-practice/contractual; ties to 54.4 disclosure period.
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 54.4 (through 1 year post-completion); ICH E6(R3) §5 (financing); contractual

key_documents: Clinical protocol + synopsis + amendments (21 CFR 312.23(a)(6), 312.30)
Investigator's Brochure (21 CFR 312.55)
IND application — Form FDA 1571 with full 312.23 content (CMC, pharm/tox, prior human experience, protocol, IB)
Form FDA 1572 Statement of Investigator (21 CFR 312.53(c)(1))
Financial disclosure Forms FDA 3454 / 3455 (21 CFR Part 54)
IRB-approved Informed Consent Form + signed ICFs (21 CFR 50)
IRB submission package + initial approval letter + continuing-review approvals (21 CFR 56)
DSMB/DMC charter + minutes (FDA 2006 DMC guidance) — conditional
Risk-based monitoring plan + monitoring visit reports (ICH E6(R3) §6.9)
Safety management/pharmacovigilance plan + IND safety reports + MedWatch 3500A (21 CFR 312.32, 312.64)
Delegation-of-authority log + site signature sheet (ICH E6(R3) §2)
CVs, medical licenses, GCP certificates (21 CFR 312.53(c)(2))
Laboratory certifications (CLIA/CAP) + normal ranges (42 CFR 493)
Clinical Trial Agreement + budget (ICH E6(R3) §5)
Site feasibility/qualification questionnaire + pre-study visit report (21 CFR 312.53(a))
Regulatory binder / Investigator Site File index (ICH E6(R3) §C.2)
Source-document templates + blank CRF/eCRF (21 CFR 312.62)
Drug/IP accountability log + destruction records (21 CFR 312.57, 312.59, 312.61, 312.62)
IND Annual Report (21 CFR 312.33)
Clinical Study Report per ICH E3
ClinicalTrials.gov registration + results (42 CFR Part 11, FDAAA 801)
Study closeout report + record-retention/archival statement (21 CFR 312.57(c), 312.62(c))

regulatory_citations.code: ICH E6(R3) Good Clinical Practice, Appendix C
regulatory_citations.title: Essential Records for the Conduct of a Clinical Trial (Step 4, adopted Jan 2025; FDA Step 2/draft at fda.gov/media/169090). Replaces E6(R2) §8; shifts 'documents' to 'records', risk-proportionate.

non_delegable_or_gating: Administration of informed consent to a patient (21 CFR 50.20/50.27) — a qualified investigator/designee must conduct the consent conversation and obtain the signature; OSSICRO may draft/version/track the ICF but must never 'consent' a subject.
IRB/IEC ethical review and approval judgment (21 CFR 56) — the continue/approve/disapprove determination is the IRB's; software prepares the submission package only.

SAE causality and expectedness determination (21 CFR 312.32, 312.64(b); ICH E2A) — clinical judgment of relatedness/seriousness/expectedness must be made by a qualified physician; OSSICRO may pre-populate MedWatch fields and enforce timelines, never adjudicate causality.
Sponsor-investigator signature attestations on Form 1571 and Form 1572 (21 CFR 312.23, 312.53) — the legal commitment must be signed by the accountable human sponsor-investigator.

Transfer of sponsor obligations (21 CFR 312.52) — obligations transfer only to a legally accountable CRO entity; a software system cannot be the transferee, so a human/entity (the micro-CRO layer or the sponsor-investigator) must hold each non-delegated function.
Authorization of investigational-product destruction and final disposition (21 CFR 312.59) — sponsor decision.
Financial-disclosure certification/disclosure content (21 CFR Part 54) — each investigator personally certifies; software collects but does not attest.
Eligibility / clinical-significance and dosing decisions per protocol — investigator medical judgment, not automatable.
Final Clinical Study Report scientific conclusions and DSMB stop/continue recommendations — human sign-off required.

briefs.topic: OSSICRO Regulatory Landscape and Citation Map — US clinical-trial authorities (21 CFR, ICH, FDA guidances, HIPAA, Common Rule) the OSSICRO generate/check/validate engine must map every generated document to
briefs.summary: OSSICRO operates in the most heavily regulated corner of US drug development: the human-subjects, IND-era clinical trial. Its generate/check/validate engine must anchor every document it produces to a specific, current authority, and must distinguish authorities that impose binding legal obligations (the 21 CFR parts and the Common Rule, which have the force of law) from those that state FDA's non-binding current thinking (ICH guidances as adopted, FDA guidance documents). The controlling structural fact is 21 CFR 312.52: sponsor obligations transfer only to a legally-accountable CRO entity, never to software — which is why OSSICRO's viable architecture is the sponsor-investigator IND (21 CFR 312.3(b)) plus a thin human-staffed micro-CRO layer for functions that legally require a transferee. The regulatory frame is in active motion: FDA published final ICH E6(R3) GCP on 2025-09-09 (no US compliance date yet set), finalized the decentralized-elements guidance on 2024-09-18, and issued its first AI-in-drug-development draft guidance on 2025-01-07 (comments closed 2025-04-07, still draft). Every OSSICRO-generated artifact must carry a citation to its governing authority and a gate that routes non-delegable judgment (consent, IRB approval, causality assessment, sponsor legal attestation) to a qualified human.

obligations_or_content.item: 21 CFR Part 11 — Electronic Records; Electronic Signatures
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 11 (subparts A general, B electronic records incl. §11.10 controls for closed systems, C electronic signatures)
obligations_or_content.detail: BINDING RULE. Governs any electronic record OSSICRO creates, modifies, maintains, or transmits that is required by an FDA predicate rule (Part 312, Part 50, Part 54, Part 56), and any electronic signature applied to them. OSSICRO must map its generate/check/validate engine, audit trail, e-signature capture (§11.50, §11.70, §11.100-11.300), and access controls (§11.10) to Part 11. Every generated e-record must carry a computer-generated, time-stamped, independent audit trail (§11.10(e)) and copies must be producible for FDA inspection. This is the single most load-bearing part for an open-source software CRO: it is the compliance boundary that makes OSSICRO's outputs admissible as regulatory records. Interpretive layer: FDA's 'Part 11 Scope and Application' guidance (2003) narrows enforcement to records that are the FDA-required record; the DCT and eSource guidances extend Part 11 thinking to remote/electronic data capture.

obligations_or_content.item: 21 CFR Part 50 — Protection of Human Subjects (Informed Consent)
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 50, esp. §50.20 (general requirements), §50.25 (elements of informed consent — basic + additional), §50.27 (documentation), §50.23-50.24 (exceptions), Subpart D §50.50-50.56 (additional protections for children)
obligations_or_content.detail: BINDING RULE. The substantive consent standard for every FDA-regulated investigation. OSSICRO generates the informed consent form (ICF) template and checks it for all required §50.25 elements, but the CONSENT ITSELF — the process of a qualified person obtaining voluntary agreement from a competent subject — is a NON-DELEGABLE human function OSSICRO must gate, never automate. Subpart D is triggered whenever a pediatric neurology patient is enrolled (directly relevant to Alton's clinical population). eConsent is permitted under §50.27 as interpreted by the 2016 joint FDA/OHRP eConsent Q&A guidance and must still satisfy Part 11 for the electronic signature.

obligations_or_content.item: 21 CFR Part 54 — Financial Disclosure by Clinical Investigators
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 54, esp. §54.4 (certification/disclosure requirement), §54.2 (definitions of disclosable financial interests — proprietary interest, significant equity, compensation affected by outcome, payments >$25,000)
obligations_or_content.detail: BINDING RULE. Requires the sponsor (or sponsor-investigator) to certify the absence of, or disclose, specified financial interests of each clinical investigator, submitted on FDA Forms 3454/3455 with the marketing application. OSSICRO must collect financial-interest data from every investigator/sub-investigator, generate the correct 3454 (certification) or 3455 (disclosure) per investigator, and validate completeness. In the sponsor-investigator model this creates a self-disclosure obligation OSSICRO must surface — the physician cannot certify away their own disclosable interest; it must be reported.

obligations_or_content.item: 21 CFR Part 56 — Institutional Review Boards
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 56, esp. §56.108 (IRB functions/operations), §56.109 (IRB review of research), §56.111 (criteria for approval), §56.115 (records), §56.110 (expedited review)
obligations_or_content.detail: BINDING RULE. Governs IRB composition, review, approval, and recordkeeping for FDA-regulated research. OSSICRO assembles the IRB submission package (protocol, ICF, investigator brochure, 1572, recruitment materials) and checks it against §56.111 approval criteria as a pre-submission readiness check — but the IRB APPROVAL DETERMINATION is the archetypal non-delegable judgment; OSSICRO must route to a registered IRB and gate on documented approval before enrollment. Pairs with the Common Rule single-IRB mandate for multi-site studies.

obligations_or_content.item: 21 CFR Part 312 — Investigational New Drug Application
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312. Subpart A §312.3 (definitions incl. sponsor, investigator, SPONSOR-INVESTIGATOR §312.3(b)), §312.7 (promotion), Subpart B §312.20-312.38 (IND content/submission incl. §312.22 general principles, §312.23 IND content and format, §312.30 protocol amendments, §312.31 information amendments, §312.32 IND SAFETY REPORTING, §312.33 annual reports), Subpart C §312.40-312.44 (IND process, clinical hold §312.42), Subpart D §312.50-312.70 (RESPONSIBILITIES — §312.50 sponsor general, §312.52 TRANSFER OF OBLIGATIONS TO A CRO, §312.53 selecting investigators/monitors, §312.55 informing investigators, §312.56 monitoring, §312.57 recordkeeping, §312.60-312.69 INVESTIGATOR responsibilities incl. §312.62 records, §312.64 reporting, §312.66 IRB assurance), Subpart E §312.80-312.88 (treatment/serious-disease), Subpart I §312.300-312.320 (EXPANDED ACCESS)
obligations_or_content.detail: BINDING RULE — the spine of OSSICRO. §312.3(b) is the legal foundation of the entire project: one physician may be BOTH sponsor and investigator (sponsor-investigator), which is how AI can absorb the coordination burden without illegally disintermediating the CRO. §312.52 is the hard constraint: sponsor obligations transfer ONLY to a legally-accountable party in a signed writing — software cannot be the transferee, which is why the thin human micro-CRO layer exists. §312.23 defines exactly what OSSICRO must assemble for the IND (cover 1571, TOC, introductory statement, general investigational plan, IB, protocols, CMC, pharm/tox, previous human experience). §312.32 (IND safety reporting) and §312.64 (investigator SAE reporting) are non-delegable causality/reporting judgments OSSICRO surfaces but must never auto-adjudicate. §312.300-320 governs the expanded-access ('compassionate use') pathway directly relevant to the guiding scenario of matching an individual patient to a treatment.

obligations_or_content.item: ICH E6(R3) — Good Clinical Practice
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E6(R3); FDA final guidance published Federal Register 2025-09-09 (90 FR / FDA-2023-D-1955); FDA doc at fda.gov/media/169090/download
obligations_or_content.detail: FDA-ADOPTED GUIDANCE (non-binding, states current thinking). The modernized GCP standard: principles-based, risk-proportionate, explicitly accommodates decentralized elements, electronic data, and computerized systems — architecturally friendly to OSSICRO. STATUS NUANCE OSSICRO MUST ENCODE: EU/EMA made E6(R3) effective 2025-07-23; FDA published final guidance 2025-09-09 but as of this writing has NOT set a formal US compliance date, so it represents FDA expectation rather than a dated mandate. Supersedes E6(R2) as the GCP reference. OSSICRO's document templates and quality checks should be built to R3 (sponsor oversight, quality-by-design, data governance, computerized-system validation Appendix) since it is the forward standard investigators will be held to.

obligations_or_content.item: ICH E8(R1) — General Considerations for Clinical Studies
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E8(R1); FDA adopted, guidance available 2022 (Federal Register 2021/2022)
obligations_or_content.detail: FDA-ADOPTED GUIDANCE. Establishes quality-by-design and the 'critical-to-quality factors' framework that E6(R3) operationalizes. Frames study design, stakeholder engagement, and fit-for-purpose data. OSSICRO maps its protocol-design templates and its risk-assessment/critical-factors checklist to E8(R1) as the upstream design-quality authority feeding the GCP conduct standard.

obligations_or_content.item: ICH E9 — Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E9 (adopted; FDA guidance 1998); addendum ICH E9(R1) on estimands and sensitivity analysis (FDA adopted 2021)
obligations_or_content.detail: FDA-ADOPTED GUIDANCE. Governs statistical design/analysis principles: prospectively defined analysis, estimands (E9(R1)), handling of missing data, multiplicity. OSSICRO's statistical-analysis-plan (SAP) template and protocol statistical sections map here. Relevant even for early-phase/Phase 2 where the estimand framework and pre-specification discipline apply. Non-delegable: the statistical judgment of a qualified biostatistician — OSSICRO drafts/structures, does not own analytic decisions.

obligations_or_content.item: ICH E2A / E2B(R3) / E2D — Safety and Pharmacovigilance
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E2A (clinical safety data management: definitions and standards for expedited reporting; FDA guidance 1997); ICH E2B(R3) (electronic transmission of ICSRs — the ICSR data element/message spec, E2B(R3) implementation); ICH E2D (post-approval safety data management)
obligations_or_content.detail: FDA-ADOPTED GUIDANCE (directly in Alton's pharmacovigilance domain). E2A defines the SAE/SUSAR concepts and expedited-reporting timelines that operationalize 21 CFR 312.32. E2B(R3) is the structured electronic ICSR format (feeds FAERS/E2B gateway and Form 3500A content). E2D covers post-marketing. OSSICRO maps its safety-reporting workflow and the FDA 3500A generation to these — but SERIOUSNESS, EXPECTEDNESS, and CAUSALITY assessment are non-delegable medical judgments OSSICRO must gate to a qualified safety physician; the engine may pre-populate and check timeliness only.

obligations_or_content.item: ICH E3 — Structure and Content of Clinical Study Reports
obligations_or_content.citation: ICH E3 (adopted; FDA guidance 1996) + E3 Q&A
obligations_or_content.detail: FDA-ADOPTED GUIDANCE. Defines the CSR structure (synopsis, methods, efficacy/safety results, appendices incl. protocol, SAP, sample CRF, investigator list). OSSICRO's end-of-study CSR template and completeness checker map to E3. Closes the documentation lifecycle from IND (312.23) through final report.

obligations_or_content.item: FDA Form FDA 1571 — Investigational New Drug Application (Cover)
obligations_or_content.citation: Form FDA 1571 (per 21 CFR 312.23(a)(1)); OMB-cleared
obligations_or_content.detail: The IND cover/transmittal form the sponsor (or sponsor-investigator) signs, listing contents and committing to sponsor obligations (not to begin until 30 days after FDA receipt, IRB compliance, GCP compliance). OSSICRO generates and completeness-checks the 1571 but the SIGNATURE is a legal attestation of sponsor responsibility — non-delegable to software; the human sponsor-investigator signs.

obligations_or_content.item: FDA Form FDA 1572 — Statement of Investigator
obligations_or_content.citation: Form FDA 1572 (per 21 CFR 312.53(c)(1)); OMB-cleared
obligations_or_content.detail: The investigator's binding commitment to conduct the study per protocol, comply with 21 CFR 312, obtain IRB review, and supervise. Lists investigator qualifications, site, IRB, and sub-investigators. OSSICRO auto-populates from CV/site data and validates required fields — but the 1572 signature is the investigator's personal legal commitment (non-delegable). In the sponsor-investigator model the same physician signs both 1571 and 1572.

obligations_or_content.item: FDA Forms FDA 3454 / FDA 3455 — Financial Disclosure
obligations_or_content.citation: Forms FDA 3454 (certification of no disclosable interests) and FDA 3455 (disclosure statement) per 21 CFR Part 54
obligations_or_content.detail: 3454 certifies the absence of the financial interests defined in §54.2; 3455 discloses them when present. Submitted with the marketing application, one per covered investigator. OSSICRO collects the financial-interest attestations and generates the correct form per investigator, flagging when a 3455 (disclosure) is required rather than a 3454.

obligations_or_content.item: FDA Form FDA 3500A — MedWatch Mandatory Reporting
obligations_or_content.citation: Form FDA 3500A (mandatory reporting by IND sponsors/manufacturers; distinct from voluntary 3500) tied to 21 CFR 312.32 and ICH E2A/E2B
obligations_or_content.detail: The mandatory adverse-event/ICSR report form for IND safety reporting. OSSICRO pre-populates 3500A from captured safety data and checks E2A timeliness (7-day fatal/life-threatening SUSAR, 15-day other serious) — but the underlying causality/expectedness determination that triggers a report is a non-delegable safety-physician judgment the engine surfaces and gates.

obligations_or_content.item: FDA Guidance — Conducting Clinical Trials With Decentralized Elements
obligations_or_content.citation: FDA final guidance, Federal Register 2024-09-18 (fda.gov/media/167696/download); finalizes May 2023 draft; DCTs also named in the 2016 21st Century Cures Act / FDORA §3606 mandate
obligations_or_content.detail: FINAL FDA GUIDANCE (non-binding). Core enabling authority for OSSICRO's operating model: telehealth visits, local-HCP tasks, remote data capture, direct-to-participant shipping. Key principle OSSICRO must encode: regulatory requirements are IDENTICAL for decentralized and traditional trials — decentralization changes logistics, not obligations. Addresses roles/responsibilities of remote personnel, local HCPs not listed on the 1572, informed consent at a distance, safety monitoring, and IP handling. This guidance is what makes a physician-operable distributed trial defensible.

obligations_or_content.item: FDA Draft Guidance — Considerations for the Use of Artificial Intelligence to Support Regulatory Decision-Making for Drug and Biological Products
obligations_or_content.citation: FDA DRAFT guidance, Federal Register 2025-01-07 (FDA-2024-D-4689); comment period closed 2025-04-07; STILL DRAFT as of this writing
obligations_or_content.detail: DRAFT FDA GUIDANCE (not final, non-binding, subject to change) — directly governs OSSICRO's AI engine when its outputs support a regulatory decision on safety/efficacy/quality. Introduces the 7-step risk-based CREDIBILITY-ASSESSMENT framework: define the regulatory question, define the AI model's context of use (COU), assess model risk (model influence x decision consequence), plan and execute credibility assessment, document. OSSICRO must (a) build its validation engine around this framework and (b) flag to users that it is DRAFT and will evolve. Note: OSSICRO's paperwork-coordination role is largely lower-risk COU (it drafts human-reviewed documents, it does not itself make the regulatory decision) — an important risk-tiering argument OSSICRO should make explicitly.

obligations_or_content.item: FDA Guidance — Use of Electronic Informed Consent: Questions and Answers
obligations_or_content.citation: Joint FDA/OHRP final guidance, December 2016 (Federal Register 2016-12-15); finalizes March 2015 draft
obligations_or_content.detail: FINAL FDA/OHRP GUIDANCE. Authorizes and conditions eConsent: electronic delivery of consent information, comprehension checks, electronic signature (must meet Part 11), IRB oversight of the eConsent materials, and subject access to a copy. OSSICRO's eConsent module maps here plus Part 50 (elements) and Part 11 (e-signature). Still gates the human consent conversation as non-delegable.

obligations_or_content.item: FDA Draft Guidance — Investigational New Drug Applications Prepared and Submitted by Sponsor-Investigators
obligations_or_content.citation: FDA DRAFT guidance, May 2015 (FDA-2015-D-1484; Federal Register 2015-05-15); note: issued as draft — verify current status before citing as final
obligations_or_content.detail: DRAFT FDA GUIDANCE tailored precisely to OSSICRO's core user: the physician who wants to run a trial but lacks regulatory infrastructure. Walks the sponsor-investigator through IND content, the review process, the dual sponsor+investigator responsibilities, and form completion (1571/1572). This is the single most on-point FDA document for OSSICRO's sponsor-investigator pathway and should anchor that wiki page. Flag its draft status.

obligations_or_content.item: FDA Expanded Access (Compassionate Use) framework
obligations_or_content.citation: 21 CFR 312 Subpart I (§312.300-312.320: §312.305 general, §312.310 individual patient IND incl. emergency, §312.315 intermediate-size, §312.320 treatment IND/protocol); FDA guidance 'Expanded Access to Investigational Drugs for Treatment Use — Questions and Answers' (updated); Form FDA 3926 (individual-patient expanded access application)
obligations_or_content.detail: BINDING RULE + GUIDANCE. Directly serves OSSICRO's guiding scenario — a clinician with a specific patient seeking an investigational treatment outside a formal trial. Subpart I sets the criteria (serious/immediately life-threatening condition, no comparable alternative, benefit justifies risk, access won't interfere with development). Form FDA 3926 is the streamlined single-patient IND request. OSSICRO can assemble the 3926 package and the required IRB concurrence and letter of authorization from the manufacturer — but the treatment decision and the manufacturer's willingness to provide the drug are non-delegable human/counterparty judgments.

obligations_or_content.item: HIPAA Privacy Rule — Reviews Preparatory to Research
obligations_or_content.citation: 45 CFR 164.512(i)(1)(ii) (preparatory-to-research reviews without authorization); with 164.512(i)(1)(i) (IRB/Privacy Board waiver of authorization), 164.508 (authorization), 164.514(e) (limited data set), 164.514(b) (de-identification)
obligations_or_content.detail: BINDING RULE. Governs how OSSICRO may touch protected health information (PHI) when a clinician searches for a matching trial for a patient. §164.512(i)(1)(ii) permits a covered entity to use PHI to review protocols/feasibility PREPARATORY to research (no PHI leaves the covered entity, review is necessary for research prep) — the cleanest lawful basis for OSSICRO's trial-matching feasibility step. Enrollment itself requires either a signed HIPAA authorization (§164.508) or a documented IRB/Privacy-Board waiver (§164.512(i)(1)(i)). OSSICRO must gate: matching = preparatory review; enrollment = authorization/waiver. This is the privacy fulcrum of the whole matching workflow.

obligations_or_content.item: Common Rule (45 CFR 46) + single-IRB mandate
obligations_or_content.citation: 45 CFR 46 Subpart A (the revised Common Rule, 2018 requirements): §46.109 (IRB review), §46.111 (approval criteria), §46.114 (COOPERATIVE RESEARCH / single-IRB mandate), §46.116 (informed consent incl. key-information requirement), §46.117 (documentation); Subparts B/C/D (pregnant women, prisoners, children); NIH single-IRB policy for multi-site studies
obligations_or_content.detail: BINDING RULE for HHS-conducted or -supported research; harmonized-but-distinct from FDA Parts 50/56. §46.114 mandates a SINGLE IRB of record for cooperative (multi-site) research — structurally important for OSSICRO's distributed/multi-site model: instead of N local IRBs, one sIRB reviews, and OSSICRO coordinates reliance agreements. §46.116 adds the 'key information' up-front-summary consent requirement and the broad-consent option. When a study is both FDA-regulated and HHS-funded, OSSICRO must satisfy BOTH the Common Rule and 21 CFR 50/56 (FDA harmonized much but not all in its 2018-2023 alignment). Subpart D (children) again triggered by pediatric neurology enrollment.

key_documents: IND application package (21 CFR 312.23) — cover Form 1571, table of contents, introductory statement, general investigational plan, investigator's brochure, protocol(s), CMC, pharmacology/toxicology, previous human experience
Form FDA 1571 (IND cover / sponsor commitment)
Form FDA 1572 (Statement of Investigator)
Form FDA 3454 (financial disclosure certification) / Form FDA 3455 (financial disclosure statement)
Form FDA 3500A (MedWatch mandatory IND safety report)
Form FDA 3926 (Individual Patient Expanded Access IND request)
Informed consent form / eConsent (21 CFR 50.25 elements; 45 CFR 46.116 key-information summary)
IRB submission package (protocol, ICF, IB, recruitment materials, 1572) and IRB approval documentation
Single-IRB reliance / authorization agreements (45 CFR 46.114)
Statistical Analysis Plan (ICH E9 / E9(R1))
Clinical Study Report (ICH E3)
IND safety reports / ICSRs (E2A timelines, E2B(R3) format)

regulatory_citations.title: Electronic Records; Electronic Signatures
regulatory_citations.relevance: Compliance boundary for every electronic record and e-signature OSSICRO generates; audit trail, validation, access controls (§11.10, §11.50-11.300).

regulatory_citations.title: Protection of Human Subjects (Informed Consent)
regulatory_citations.relevance: Substantive consent standard (§50.25 elements, §50.27 documentation, Subpart D children); consent process is non-delegable, gated.

regulatory_citations.title: Financial Disclosure by Clinical Investigators
regulatory_citations.relevance: Drives Forms 3454/3455; self-disclosure obligation in the sponsor-investigator model.

regulatory_citations.title: Institutional Review Boards

regulatory_citations.relevance: HHS-funded research standard; single-IRB-of-record for multi-site studies; §46.116 key-information consent; Subpart D children. Must be satisfied alongside FDA Parts 50/56 when dual-regulated.

non_delegable_or_gating: Obtaining informed consent from a patient (the consent conversation and voluntary agreement) — OSSICRO drafts/checks the ICF and runs eConsent mechanics, never substitutes for the human consent process (21 CFR 50.20-50.27; 45 CFR 46.116)
IRB/ethics-committee approval determination (21 CFR 56.109-56.111; 45 CFR 46.109-46.111) — OSSICRO assembles and pre-checks the submission; the IRB judgment is theirs
SAE / SUSAR seriousness, expectedness, and CAUSALITY assessment (21 CFR 312.32/312.64; ICH E2A) — a qualified safety physician's determination; OSSICRO pre-populates 3500A and checks timeliness only
Sponsor and investigator legal attestations — signatures on Forms 1571 and 1572 (21 CFR 312.23(a)(1), 312.53(c)(1)) commit the human to statutory responsibility
Transfer of sponsor obligations to a legally-accountable CRO entity in signed writing (21 CFR 312.52) — the human micro-CRO layer, not the software, is the transferee
Statistical analysis decisions / estimand selection (ICH E9/E9(R1)) — qualified biostatistician owns; OSSICRO structures the SAP
Financial-disclosure certification of the investigator's own interests (21 CFR 54.4) — a self-attestation the physician makes; OSSICRO cannot certify it away
The clinician's medical treatment decision and the manufacturer's decision to provide an investigational drug in expanded access (21 CFR 312 Subpart I) — human/counterparty judgments

AI-model regulatory-decision reliance beyond documented, human-reviewed COU — per FDA 2025 AI draft guidance credibility framework, high-influence/high-consequence uses require human oversight and pre-agreed credibility evidence

sweeps.scope: Set 1 institutional sweep: Dana-Farber, Harvard (HMS/Harvard Catalyst), MIT, Stanford (Spectrum/SCTR), UCSF CTSI, Johns Hopkins (ICTR), Duke (DCRI/DUHS), MD Anderson, Memorial Sloan Kettering, Broad Institute. Search-engine-depth pass (WebSearch + WebFetch); several institutional pages 403'd to WebFetch (Hopkins hopkinsmedicine.org, ICTR study-team-resources) and are noted as such rather than fabricated. Bonus finds outside the 10 (UNC Lineberger, U Pitt, Ohio State CTSI) flagged separately since they materially strengthen the IND-template corpus.

institutions.name: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute
institutions.resource_title: Clinical Trials Business Office (research administration)
institutions.what_it_offers: Describes DFCI's Clinical Trials Business Office, which negotiates research agreements/budgets and supports IIT conduct, but no publicly downloadable IIT toolkit, IND guide, or template set surfaced. Phone: 617-632-6144.
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: Low direct reuse — no public templates found. Useful only as a reference example that a top cancer center centralizes IIT budget/agreement negotiation in a CTO function distinct from the PI; OSSICRO's micro-CRO layer should model this separation.

institutions.name: Harvard Catalyst (Harvard Clinical and Translational Science Center)
institutions.resource_title: IND/IDE Consulting Program and case-study library
institutions.what_it_offers: Free consult service for sponsor-investigators and IRBs across Harvard-affiliated hospitals + partner institutions. Publication library includes: 'Sharing Sponsor-Investigator IND/IDE Resources: A Contributory Network Model' (describes the Massachusetts multi-institution shared-service model), 'Sponsor-Investigator FAQs' (PDF), 'Physician Checklist to Obtain Expanded Access IND for Individual Patient', and case studies: 'An Investigator's Responsibilities', 'FDA Inspection of an IND Study', 'Investigator-Held IDE', 'Sharing an IND', 'Why Institutions Do Not Hold INDs'.
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: High. 'Why Institutions Do Not Hold INDs' and 'Sharing an IND' are directly on-point for OSSICRO's core legal thesis (sponsor-investigator IND under 21 CFR 312.3, institutions structurally avoid holding INDs). The contributory-network-model paper is a precedent for a shared regulatory-support layer across otherwise-independent PIs — structurally analogous to what OSSICRO's micro-CRO layer does in software. The Expanded Access checklist is a reusable pattern for a 'generate/check' single-patient IND wizard.

institutions.name: MIT (COUHES)
institutions.resource_title: COUHES Forms & Templates
institutions.what_it_offers: IRB-side forms: ICF template, assent template (ages 8-17), waiver/alteration-of-consent request, HIPAA authorization for PHI release, protocol event/deviation reporting form, continuing review questionnaire, protocol amendment application, financial-interest disclosure supplement, DoD scientific-validity review form, genomic data-sharing (NIH GDS) certification. No IND/IDE-specific or sponsor-investigator delegation-log templates found on this page — MIT's COUHES is a pure human-subjects/IRB office, not a clinical trials regulatory-affairs shop (MIT has no hospital; MIT-affiliated trials route IND/IDE support through Harvard Catalyst per the Massachusetts shared-service model above).
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: Medium — reusable as a baseline ICF/assent/deviation-report template set and confirms the 8-17 assent age-band convention. Confirms architecturally that IND/IDE regulatory-affairs function and IRB-ethics function are organizationally distinct even at peer institutions — supports OSSICRO's entity separation (IRB/ethics vs. sponsor-investigator regulatory-affairs vs. safety board).

institutions.name: Stanford Medicine (Spectrum / Stanford Center for Clinical and Translational Research, SCTR)
institutions.resource_title: IND/IDE Tools and Templates (Clinical Research Quality, CRQ)
institutions.what_it_offers: Templates for: Regulatory Binder Table of Contents (IND studies) and (IDE studies) — i.e., a delegation-log/regulatory-binder organizing structure; IND Application template; IDE Application template. Distributed via Stanford Box; parent service page (https://med.stanford.edu/spectrum/researcher-resources/clinical-research-quality-crq/ind_ide-and-regulatory-affairs-services.html) also references 'Regulatory Knowledge and Support (RKS)' offering GCP/Stanford-policy consultation distinct from CRQ. Protocol templates (NIH/Stanford formats) referenced separately via Stanford's Research Management Group (https://med.stanford.edu/rmg/clinical-trials/study-set-up/protocol0.html). ClinicalTrials.gov registration quick-guide (Doc# FAQ-004 Rev 02) also published.
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: High. The Regulatory Binder Table of Contents is exactly the artifact OSSICRO's 'generate' engine should produce automatically per-trial (maps directly to the delegation-log/regulatory-binder deliverable named in the task). Confirms a 3-way split (CRQ=regulatory templates, RKS=GCP/policy consult, RMG=protocol/registration) that OSSICRO's entity model should mirror.

institutions.name: UCSF (Clinical and Translational Science Institute, CTSI)
institutions.resource_title: CTSI Clinical Trial Design Studio
institutions.what_it_offers: An intensive consultation program (not a static template library) aimed at improving IIT quality pre-launch. UCSF also participates in the NCATS Trial Innovation Network (TIN), giving investigators access to multi-site IIT infrastructure built by other CTSA hubs (https://ctsi.ucsf.edu/about-us/programs/trial-innovation-network). No standalone public IND/sponsor-investigator template library surfaced at ctsi.ucsf.edu in this pass.
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: Medium — the Design Studio model (structured human consultation gate before trial launch) is a good analog for an OSSICRO 'pre-flight review' step that surfaces non-delegable decision points to a human (PI, IRB chair) before the software proceeds, consistent with the HARD LINE constraint.

institutions.name: Johns Hopkins (Institute for Clinical and Translational Research, ICTR)
institutions.resource_title: Drug and Device Resource Service (DDRS) + New Investigator Toolkit
institutions.what_it_offers: DDRS explicitly guides investigators/sponsors/sponsor-investigators through securing institutional approval to serve as sponsor-investigator and submitting an IND/IDE to FDA; offers a DDRS Consultation Service (https://ictr.johnshopkins.edu/service/drug-device/ddrs/consultation/) and template artifacts including an 'IND Annual Progress Report Guidance and Template for Drug Products' (v3.0). Separately, Hopkins IRB publishes a 'New Investigator Toolkit' (hopkinsmedicine.org/institutional-review-board/new-investigator-toolkit) and ICTR publishes an Investigational Drug Services role document (https://ictr.johnshopkins.edu/wp-content/uploads/2019-IDS-Final-.pdf). Both hopkinsmedicine.org and the ICTR study-team-resources page returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch in this pass — content summarized from search-index snippets only, not fetched full-text; flag for manual re-verification before citing specifics.
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: High in concept, unverified in detail. The DDRS institutional-approval-to-become-sponsor-investigator gate is a concrete precedent for an OSSICRO workflow step ('does this institution/PI need internal sign-off before FDA submission'). The IND Annual Progress Report template is a recurring-compliance-document type OSSICRO's engine should generate/track (FDA-mandated annual report under 21 CFR 312.33).

institutions.name: Duke University (School of Medicine / DUHS, Office of Regulatory Affairs and Quality; DCRI separately)
institutions.resource_title: IND and IDE Sponsor and Investigator Training Modules (ORAQ)
institutions.what_it_offers: Two ~1-hour training modules (LMS/HTML/PDF formats, video + 10-question assessment): 'IND Sponsor and Investigator Training' (drugs/biologics) and 'IDE Sponsor and Investigator Training' (devices), mandatory for Duke faculty holding an IND/IDE as sponsor or sponsor-investigator. Notably made available to outside academic institutions at no cost via the ReGARDD collaborative (Regulatory Support for Clinical Research web/consortium; request form on page). Contact: ORAQ-ApplicationRegistration@duke.edu. Duke Clinical Research Institute (DCRI, dcri.org) is a separate, much larger academic research organization running industry-scale trials — no public IIT template library found there; DCRI functions more like a contract research organization than a template-publishing CTO.
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: High. The Duke/ReGARDD training modules are the most directly reusable EXTERNALLY-LICENSED artifact found in this pass — explicitly free to non-Duke academic institutions. OSSICRO should request access and consider embedding/linking as the canonical sponsor-investigator-responsibilities training layer rather than authoring one from scratch. DCRI's CRO-like structure is a real-world existence proof that a sponsor-investigator's coordination burden scales into something CRO-shaped — supporting OSSICRO's 'micro-CRO operating layer' concept.

institutions.name: MD Anderson Cancer Center
institutions.resource_title: IND Office (Office Task Force) and IIT audit function
institutions.what_it_offers: Confirms MD Anderson operates a dedicated 'IND Office' (a task force existed 2017-2018 per a faculty bio) that monitors data quality and patient safety for interventional IITs, auditing each IIT-held IND at least annually via an IND Clinical Research Quality Specialist. No public-facing toolkit, SOP set, or template library was located in this pass; MD Anderson's regulatory materials appear to be internal-access-only.
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: Low direct template reuse (nothing public found), but the annual-audit-per-IIT-IND cadence is a concrete, citable operational precedent OSSICRO's 'validate' engine should implement as a recurring compliance check for any sponsor-investigator IND it tracks.

institutions.name: Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center
institutions.resource_title: Data and Safety Monitoring Plan (DSMP) — NCI Cancer Centers Program library copy
institutions.what_it_offers: MSK's institutional DSMP is on file with NCI's Cancer Centers Program (this is the standard mechanism: NCI-designated cancer centers must file an institution-wide DSMP as part of their Cancer Center Support Grant). Full-text parse failed in this pass (binary/compressed PDF stream not decodable by the fetch tool) — content not verified beyond confirming the document exists and is NCI-published; re-fetch with a PDF-native reader before citing structural specifics (risk-tiered monitoring levels, DSMB composition, SAE-reporting timelines). MSK also references a 'Clinical Research Portal' internally for SOPs/forms, not public.
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: High as a document TYPE (every NCI-designated cancer center must have one; this is the canonical model for OSSICRO's own risk-tiered DSMP template — monitoring level scaled to trial risk, independent DSMB triggers, SAE reporting lines) — but the actual MSK content needs re-extraction (binary PDF) before OSSICRO cites specific thresholds from it. Recommend re-fetching via a PDF text extractor rather than WebFetch's markdown converter.

institutions.name: Broad Institute
institutions.resource_title: No clinical-trials/IIT toolkit found; institution type mismatch
institutions.what_it_offers: The Broad Institute is a genomics/basic-science research institute (MIT/Harvard-affiliated), not a hospital or clinical trials site. It runs a CLIA-certified Clinical Research Sequencing Platform (CRSP) for genomic assay development, and an Office of Sponsored Research for grants administration — neither publishes IIT/IND toolkits, CTO SOPs, or sponsor-investigator guides. No human-subjects clinical trials office analogous to the other 9 institutions was found.
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: None directly. Worth noting in the wiki as a deliberate scope exclusion (Broad has no IIT-support infrastructure to mine) rather than a gap in the research pass, so a future auditor doesn't re-run this search expecting different results.

institutions.name: University of Pittsburgh (bonus, not in original 10 — surfaced via search)
institutions.resource_title: IND/IDE Support: Investigational New Drug (IND) Templates
institutions.what_it_offers: The most complete public IND document-template inventory found in this entire sweep: Initial IND Application body, Clinical Study Protocol section, CMC (Chemistry Manufacturing and Controls) section, Pharmacology/Toxicology section; Annual Report, Safety Report, Withdrawal/Final Report; Protocol Amendment templates (New Investigator / New Protocol / Change in Protocol); Information Amendment; Change of Sponsor; Change of Sponsor-Investigator; Meeting Request; Inactive Status Request; Concurrence of Exemption Request; and a Certification/Disclosure of Financial Interest of Clinical Investigators form (FDA Form 3454/3455 equivalent).
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: Very high — this maps almost 1:1 onto the FDA-mandated IND lifecycle document set under 21 CFR 312 (Subpart B: 312.23 initial application contents, 312.30 protocol amendments, 312.32 IND safety reports, 312.33 annual reports, 312.38 withdrawal). Strong candidate as the skeleton for OSSICRO's core IND template library; verify current license/terms directly with Pitt's Human Subjects Protection Office before reuse.

institutions.name: UNC Lineberger Comprehensive Cancer Center (bonus — surfaced via search)
institutions.resource_title: Investigator-Initiated Trials (IIT) Toolkit
institutions.what_it_offers: A full public IIT lifecycle toolkit with named sections: Getting Started, Finding Funding, LOI Development/Submission, Initial Protocol Development (with Templates/Forms subsection), Amendments, Continued FDA Maintenance, Investigator's Brochure (with an Investigator's Brochure Template (Word), SOP-30 'Drafting and Amending of Investigator's Brochures', and a companion Work Instruction-30), Sponsor Oversight Requirements for IITs, Other Players (stakeholder roles), Investigator Training, and an RFA for High-Enrolling Clinical Trial Operational Support. Contact: LCCC_IND@unc.edu.
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: Very high — this is the single best-structured public IIT toolkit found in the entire sweep and should be treated as a reference information architecture for OSSICRO's own wiki table of contents (its section order — funding to LOI to protocol to amendments to IB to sponsor-oversight to stakeholder-roles to training — is close to the OSSICRO document-flow model). The paired SOP + Work Instruction pattern (policy statement vs. step-by-step execution doc) is a reusable documentation convention worth adopting directly.

institutions.name: Ohio State University CTSI (bonus — surfaced via search)
institutions.resource_title: Investigator-Initiated Clinical Research Guidebook/Checklists
institutions.what_it_offers: A single consolidated PDF guidebook framed explicitly around the dual sponsor/investigator burden ('significant [additional] tasks... than a study designed and initiated by industry'), with regulatory role definitions (Investigator, Sponsor, Sponsor-Investigator) drawn from 21 CFR 312.3.
institutions.usefulness_to_ossicro: Medium — useful as a plain-language explainer of the sponsor/investigator/sponsor-investigator distinction for OSSICRO's onboarding/education layer, less useful as a template source (guidebook format, not a document library).

key_insights: Convergent institutional pattern: at every research-hospital-affiliated institution surveyed (Harvard, Stanford, Hopkins, Duke, UNC), IND/IDE sponsor-investigator regulatory-affairs support is organizationally SEPARATE from the IRB/ethics function and from general clinical-trials-office (protocol/budget/registration) support — typically a 3-way split (regulatory-affairs consult service; IRB/ethics; CTO/protocol-ops). OSSICRO's entity model (SPONSOR/INVESTIGATOR, IRB, DSMB, pharmacovigilance as distinct roles) mirrors real institutional practice, not an artificial abstraction.

Harvard Catalyst's 'Why Institutions Do Not Hold INDs' and 'Sharing an IND' case studies are direct, citable, named-institution confirmation of OSSICRO's core legal thesis: institutions structurally decline to hold INDs, and the sponsor-investigator role under 21 CFR 312.3 is the load-bearing legal mechanism, not an edge case.

The Massachusetts multi-institution 'contributory network model' (Harvard Catalyst) and Duke's ReGARDD program (free cross-institutional training modules) are both real precedents for shared/networked regulatory-support infrastructure across otherwise-independent sponsor-investigators — directly analogous to OSSICRO's open-source-plus-thin-micro-CRO-layer design, and both are candidates for direct partnership/licensing conversations rather than reinvention.

University of Pittsburgh's IND template inventory maps almost exactly onto the FDA IND lifecycle document set defined in 21 CFR 312 Subpart B (initial application, protocol amendments 312.30, safety reports 312.32, annual reports 312.33, withdrawal 312.38) — this is the strongest single candidate skeleton for OSSICRO's core template library found in this pass.

UNC Lineberger's public IIT toolkit is the best-structured complete public reference architecture found (paired SOP + Work Instruction convention, full lifecycle section ordering) and should be used as an information-architecture model for OSSICRO's own wiki, separate from its value as a template source.

MD Anderson's annual per-IIT IND audit cadence (via a dedicated IND Clinical Research Quality Specialist) is a concrete operational precedent for OSSICRO's 'validate' engine to implement as a recurring, scheduled compliance check rather than a one-time submission check.

Two fetch failures need re-verification before any content is cited in the wiki: (1) hopkinsmedicine.org and ictr.johnshopkins.edu study-team-resources both returned HTTP 403 to automated WebFetch — Hopkins content above is search-snippet-derived only, not full-text-verified; (2) the MSK DSMP PDF (NCI Cancer Centers Program mirror) is a binary/compressed PDF stream that the fetch tool could not parse into text — existence confirmed, structural content (monitoring tiers, DSMB composition) NOT verified and should not be cited until re-extracted with a proper PDF reader.

Broad Institute is a genuine scope-exclusion, not a research gap: it is a genomics/basic-science institute with no hospital, no IIT infrastructure, and no sponsor-investigator support function to document. Worth stating explicitly in the wiki so it isn't re-searched later expecting different results.

publications.citation: Rubin EH, et al. Harvard Catalyst. 'Sharing Sponsor-Investigator IND/IDE Resources: A Contributory Network Model of Scalable, Adaptable Support Services Across Massachusetts Academic Institutions.'
publications.takeaway: Describes a real, functioning shared-services model across multiple Massachusetts academic institutions for sponsor-investigator IND/IDE support — direct precedent for a networked (rather than single-institution) regulatory support layer.

publications.takeaway: Peer-reviewed description of the IND/IDE consult service model; useful primary-source citation for OSSICRO's rationale on why a shared regulatory-consult layer is both feasible and precedented at academic medical centers.
